chunks.text: MS in Applied Data Science Welcome to the Events & Deadlines page for the MS in Applied Data Science program! Here, you'll find all the latest updates, upcoming events, important deadlines, and additional news related to our exciting program. Stay informed and engaged with everything happening in the world of data science.

In this Data Science Seminar Series, you will hear from MS-ADS alumni Steve Veldman, who is a Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Rotational Labs. The Seminar Series presents projects, research, and questions posed by our instructors. Learn how UChicago does data science, while seeing how our instructors and students set us apart.
path: Data Science Seminar Series – An Introduction to Computer Vision Techniques and Applications

chunks.text: Speaker Bio : A veteran of the manufacturing industry, Steve Veldman is passionate about building practical and impactful solutions to real-world problems. In his role as a Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Rotational Labs, Steve helps to make the power of machine learning and artificial intelligence accessible to organizations of all sizes by building a meaningful understanding of his clients’ goals and challenges. He specializes in computer vision as well as classical machine learning techniques, and has a keen interest in the developing field of multimodal AI. Outside of work, Steve enjoys playing guitar, running, hiking, and cultivating an eclectic vinyl record collection.
path: Data Science Seminar Series – An Introduction to Computer Vision Techniques and Applications

chunks.text: Presentation Summary: This webinar breaks down the fundamental tasks of computer vision, explaining the mechanics behind how neural networks, transformers, and the latest vision-language models (VLMs) interpret visual data. We’ll explore real-world applications, demonstrating how specific CV techniques can be applied to use cases as complex and varied as interpreting medical images, monitoring security camera feeds, and digitizing paper documents. We will also discuss the exciting shift toward multimodal LLMs and how they are expanding what’s possible in visual reasoning. Whether you are looking to understand the core technology or its practical impact on the field, this session will provide a strong foundation for navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of computer vision.
path: Data Science Seminar Series – An Introduction to Computer Vision Techniques and Applications

chunks.text: Attend this event to receive an application fee waiver for the MS in Applied Data Science program. After the session, you’ll receive a code via email that can be applied once your application is submitted.
path: Data Science Seminar Series – An Introduction to Computer Vision Techniques and Applications

chunks.text: Ready to explore an advanced degree in a rapidly growing field? Join us on May 13th for a virtual information session featuring Senior Assistant Director of Enrollment Patrick Vonesh and Associate Clinical Professor Nick Kadochnikov to learn more about the online Master’s in Applied Data Science program. This session is designed to provide valuable insights into the program’s curriculum, admission requirements, scholarship opportunities, and more!
path: Info Session – MS in Applied Data Science (Online Program)

All program offerings, including the part-time online program, begin in Autumn. Curious about the academic calendar? Please use the following link to access current and future calendar years: Academic Calendar
We are excited to announce that the application portal for the Master’s in Applied Data Science is now open!
With the application portal now open, we encourage prospective applicants to take the following steps:

- Update your resume to reflect your most recent experiences and achievements.
- Connect with your recommenders to ensure you secure strong letters of recommendation.
- Schedule a conversation with Patrick Vonesh and/or Jose Alvarado to learn more about the program and discuss your goals.
- Attend one of our upcoming, virtual events.

In-Person:
- January 26, 2026 – International Application Deadline (requiring visa sponsorship from UChicago)
- March 4, 2026 – Second Priority Application Deadline
- May 6, 2026 – Third Priority Application Deadline
- June 23, 2026 – Final Application Deadline

- International applicants who require a new visa and did not apply by the January 2026 deadline must plan to apply in the next application cycle for Autumn 2027 entry.
- Applicants who are currently studying in the United States on an F-1 visa and will transfer their SEVIS record from another U.S. institution may still apply by the remaining application deadlines.
Online application decisions are released on a rolling basis. The deadline represents the last date applications are accepted for the respective start date.
*The application portal may close early if the cohort is filled.
